A kind of method that selective laser fusing prepares TiAl/TC4 microlaminate composites

The present invention provides a kind of method that selective laser fusing prepares TiAl/TC4 microlaminate composites, belongs to advanced manufacturing technology field, comprising: pretreatment formed powder；Slicing treatment constitutional detail model, cooks up scan path and parameter；Pre- hot substrate；Selective laser melting unit alternately lays formed powder, laser successively scans and forms drip molding.Have the beneficial effect that successively replacing accumulation by selective laser melting unit prepares microdisk Lasers.Basal plate preheating is utilized simultaneously, and laser in-situ, which is pre-sintered, reduces temperature gradient, improves residualinternal stress, effectively inhibits the generation of crackle, has obtained complete fine and close, the micro- lamination sample of the preferable TiAl/TC4 of comprehensive performance；Different number of plies ratios can be set according to performance requirement, the preparation of different performance demand drip molding is realized with this, reduce the limitation of the intensity, plasticity and density of drip molding to preparation method, while also extending the application field of SLM.

A component for an aero engine, an aero engine module comprising such a component, and method of manufacturing said component by additive manufacturing

A component (20) for a module (5.1,5.2) of an aero engine (1), comprising an outer shell segment (21) defining a segment of a gas channel (11) radially outwards, a plurality of vane stage segments (8.1) arranged axially consecutive, each vane stage segment (8.1) comprising a plurality of vanes (22) arranged circumferentially consecutive, wherein the vanes (22) are formed integrally with the outer shell segment (21). A module for an aero engine with such components and method for manufacturing the component by additive manufacturing are also provided.

Gpu material assignment for 3d printing using 3d distance fields

A method includes selecting (112) a voxel (420) from a three-dimensional build space (400) and for the selected voxel, determining a distance field value relative to a digital model of a 3D part (402) in the three-dimensional build space. The distance field value is then used to select (904) at least one material selection rule (220) and a feature of the voxel is applied (906) to the at least one material selection rule to identify a material designation (228) for the voxel. The material designation indicates no material is to be placed at the voxel when the material selection rule identifies no material for the voxel and the material designation indicates at least one material is to be placed at the voxel when the at least one material selection rule identifies the at least one material for the voxel. The material designation for the voxel is then output (126) for use in printing the 3D part using an additive manufacturing system.

Preparation of elastomer seal, useful e.g. in engine, comprises molding stereolithography part/model element, opening the mold, pouring elastomer sealing, polymerizing cast material and removing silicon rubber and/or polyurethane

Preparation of elastomer seals comprises producing a stereolithography part or model element, molding the stereolithography part or model element for production of a mold for one or more elastomer seals with cast material for the mold and harnesses of the cast material, opening the mold and taking the stereo lithography part or model element, pouring the elastomer sealing, polymerizing the cast material in the mold, and removing one or more silicon rubber and/or polyurethane comprising elastomer seal (exhibiting high stability opposite to oils and fuels), from the mold. Preparation of elastomer seals comprises producing a stereo lithography part or model element, molding the stereo lithography part or model element for production of a mold for one or more elastomer seals with cast material for the mold and harnesses of this cast material, opening the mold and taking the stereo lithography part or model element, pouring the elastomer sealing, polymerizing the cast material in the mold, and removing one or more silicon rubber and/or polyurethane comprising elastomer seal from the mold, where the cast material produces from the production of the elastomer sealing by a vacuum casting procedure into the mold and the casting material comprises at least a casting material for the elastomer sealing comprising pourable group, with ambient temperature vulcanizing multi-component mixture for the production of silicon rubber and pourable mixture for the production of polyurethane, and the elastomer seals exhibit high stability opposite to oils and fuels. An independent claim is included for an elastomer seal made up of at least one cast material.
Additive Manufacturing

Incorporating surface-modified components in additively manufactured components

An additive manufacturing assembly may include a surface-modified component to be incorporated into an additively manufactured component by adhering the at least one component to the additively manufactured component during an additive manufacturing technique. The surface-modified component includes a modified surface that is modified to have increased adhesion to the additively manufactured component. The additive manufacturing assembly also includes means for additively forming layers of material using the additive manufacturing technique and a computing device, the computing device is configured to control the means for additively forming layers to form a layer of material on the surface of the surface-modified component and control the means for additively forming layers to form, on the layer of material, at least one additional layer of material to form the additively manufactured component incorporating the surface-modified component.

A three-dimensional printer head

The invention relates to a three-dimensional printer head (10) comprising a receiving section (15) having at least two inlets (17,19) for receiving material into the receiving section, wherein the printer head further comprises a dispensing unit (21) for printing a three dimensional product, an extrusion screw (13) at least extending between the receiving section and the dispensing unit for conditioning the material received in the receiving section and for displacing the material to the dispensing unit for dispensing the conditioned material.

3D printer with shockproof base

The invention discloses a 3D printer with a shockproof base, and relates to the field of printers. According to the invention, the problems that the machine body of the existing 3D printer is thickened and enlarged in order to improve stability, the 3D printer with larger size is heavier, and the 3D printer is inconvenient to carry and move are solved by arranging the base, the connecting seat, the bracket, the guide wheel and the U-shaped frame.

Additive manufacturing method and additive manufacturing apparatus

To provide an additive manufacturing method and an additive manufacturing apparatus, capable of forming metal-molded objects in various shapes, each including a porous region, efficiently through a few steps.SOLUTION: This invention provides an additive manufacturing apparatus 2 and an additive manufacturing method using the same, the apparatus including: a head 30 with a supply part 20 that supplies metal powder, together with a carrier gas, to an irradiation part 10 that irradiates an irradiation area S with laser light and an irradiation area S, and a movement mechanism 40 that relatively moves the head 30 and a molded region 60, wherein a metal hydride powder is blended in a metal powder, thereby forming a molded object 50 containing a porous region in the molded region 60.SELECTED DRAWING: Figure 1

Apparatus for production of three-dimensional objects by stereolithography

A system for making three dimensional objects. The system may have a frame, a build platform movably coupled to the frame, a vat removably secured so that the build platform may be lowered within the vat, and a movable radiation source capable of movement along an x and y-axis. The vat may have a radiation permeable flat bottom. The build platform may have a work surface disposed thereon. There may be at least one stepper motor capable of moving the radiation source, the build platform, or both the radiation source and the build platform. There may also be a power source, a data reader device and or computer communication device, and a microcontroller in communication with the at least one stepper motor and at least one of the data reader device or computer communication device.

Method and system for additive manufacturing of peelable sacrificial structures

A method of making a laminate of a three-dimensional object, the method comprising (i) a model layer stack arranged in a pattern constructed corresponding to the shape of the object and made of modeling material, and (ii) a sacrificial layer stack made of elastomeric material Sequentially distributing and solidifying a plurality of layers comprising a structure and (iii) an interlayer stack made of a support material having a modulus of elasticity less than the elastomeric material and present between the model layer stack and the sacrificial structure; And applying a peel force to the sacrificial structure (eg, in a dry environment) to remove the sacrificial structure and expose the model layer stack and / or the interlayer stack below the sacrificial structure.

A kind of 3 D-printing biologic medical insole and Method of printing

The invention discloses a kind of 3 D-printing biologic medical insole and Method of printings, including at least three Foot pressure tress concentration zones A, B, C；Wherein, Foot pressure tress size is ordered as A > B > C；Several stomatas are filled in each Foot pressure tress concentration zones, and the porosity size in A, B, C is incremented by successively, the density in A, B, C is successively successively decreased.Medical scanning is carried out to the foot of patient before designing insole, then three-dimensional modeling is carried out to the human foot model of scanning, mechanical analysis is carried out to threedimensional model again, the inner porosity that density is controllable in insole is designed according to mechanical analysis result, insole model is formed, electrostatic spinning process printout is finally utilized.The configuration of the present invention is simple is novel, and material is light and handy, and precision is high, in particular, the porous structure that insole internal density is controllable, the case where capable of being directed to patient because of foot suiting measures to different conditions, allows patient over the course for the treatment of, reduces painful.

Method for producing a three-dimensional object using the fusen origami technique

A method for producing a three-dimensional object using the fusen origami technique, characterized in that on a flat blank prepared so as to acquire internal volume when filled, said blank having four larger outer corners and two smaller opposing outer corners, a fold is made along a line between the ends of the sides of one of the smaller corners and a fold is made along a line between the ends of the sides of the other smaller corner. The result is that of facilitating the production of three-dimensional objects, providing greater possibilities for using different materials, and widening the applicability of producing three-dimensional objects using the fusen origami technique, by virtue of the fact that a flat blank prepared so as to acquire internal volume when filled, said blank having four larger outer corners and two smaller opposing outer corners, can be opened by the application of a force to fold points which allow for the formation of optimal ribs as the blank is opened, said fold points lying along lines between the ends of the sides of one of the smaller corners and along lines between the ends of the sides of the other smaller corner.

Method for automatic identification of material deposition deficiencies during an additive manufacturing process and manufacturing device

The present invention pertains to a 3D-printing method and a manufacturing device configured to execute such a method. Modelling material is deposited in powder form, melted with an energy beam and subsequently solidified into solid form. According to the invention, each deposited material layer is examined by looking at a radiation signature of electromagnetic radiation emitted and/or reflected from a melt spot, which is created by the energy beam during melting of the deposited modelling material. The radiation signature discriminates between modelling material in powder form and solid form. This makes it possible to assess if the modelling material is insufficiently deposited at the melt spot in powder form. In case a material deposition deficiency is determined, a suitable corrective action may be performed, e.g. redepositing modelling material within the last deposited material layer. 
     The present invention relates particularly to selective laser melting (SLM) and other powder bed fusion processes. However, the present concepts are not limited to SLM and may be applied to various other additive manufacturing methods. The solution of the present invention advantageously provides an effective automatized solution for identifying material deposition deficiencies arising during manufacture.

Method and device for producing three-dimensional objects

A method for producing a three-dimensional object layer by layer using a powdered material which can be solidified by irradiating it with at least two electron beams, the method comprising a preheating step, wherein the preheating step comprises the following sub-steps: scanning the pre-heated powder layer area (100) by scanning a first electron beam in a first zone (I) distributed over the pre-heated powder layer area (100) and by scanning a second electron beam in a second zone (II) distributed over the pre-heated powder layer area (100), wherein successive scanning paths are separated by at least a safety distance (ay), the sub-step further comprising the steps of: when simultaneously preheating the powder material in the first and second zones, respectively, the preheating of the first and second electron beams is synchronized such that the first and second electron beams are always separated from each other by at least a minimum safety distance (Δ X).

The system and method that the increasing material manufacturing of component is tracked and managed

The system and method for being tracked and being managed the present invention relates to the increasing material manufacturing to component.In one embodiment, this method includes keeping the digital contract between registered product owner and registered increasing material manufacturing quotient formalized.The number contract includes the mark for every registered increasing material manufacturing printer that the reference identification of the design document of the product, the registered increasing material manufacturing quotient possess and the product manufacturing quantity that is licensed.This method further includes making: after the number contract is formalized, the license for accessing the design document is provided to the registered increasing material manufacturing quotient；And it records from every registered received printing Transaction Information of increasing material manufacturing printer.The printing Transaction Information includes by the quantity of the product of the registered increasing material manufacturing printer manufacture.This method further includes or when the digital contract is breached, terminating the digital contract by the license that revocation accesses the design document when manufactured product quantity is equal to the product manufacturing quantity being licensed.

A kind of the 3D printing manufacturing method and application of metal tantalum

The present invention relates to a kind of 3D printing manufacturing method of metal tantalum and applications, belong to increases material manufacturing technology field.A kind of 3D printing manufacturing method of metal tantalum, it include: that spherical tantalum powder is packed into 3D printing former, oxygen content is purged into 3D printing former less than 100ppm to 3D printing former using argon gas, TC4 titanium alloy substrate is preheated under conditions of temperature is 90~150 DEG C, scanning interlayer angle is 67 °, carries out 3D printing and is manufactured.The Ta powder that the manufacturing method uses is spherical in shape, with high purity, oxygen content is low, mobile performance is excellent, the metal tantalum surface roughness R of 3D printing forming preparation a ≤ 5.0 μm, consistency ρ >=99.8%, tensile strength sigma b =697MPa, yield strength σ 0.2 =581MPa, elongation percentage δ=27.5% can reach the requirement of ISO13782 biologic medical field surgical implant performance indicator.

3d printing apparatus for manufacturing cement product and mehtod thereof

The present invention provides a three-dimensional printed cement product manufacturing apparatus and method for manufacturing the same, which can prevent premature curing of cement mortar by separately supplying a mixed material for producing cement mortar in a state in which the quick-setting agent does not react It has its main purpose.   According to an aspect of the present invention, there is provided an apparatus for manufacturing a three-dimensional printed cement product, comprising: a first supply unit for supplying a first material including cement and water; A second supply unit for supplying a second material including sand and quick-setting; A mixer for receiving the first material and the second material to produce a cement mortar; A nozzle connected to a lower portion of the mixer to discharge the cement mortar; And a transfer part for moving the nozzle in a three-dimensional space.

Method of investment casting using additive manufacturing

The present disclosure provides a method of investment casting using additive manufacturing. In one aspect, the method includes: creating a digital model of a building component using one or more software tools executed on a computer device and importing the digital model in an additive manufacturing apparatus; producing a first physical specimen by controlling the additive manufacturing apparatus in accordance with the digital model; creating a first negative mold using the first physical specimen as a template, the first negative mold having a hollow space substantially defined by a surface profile of the first physical specimen; producing a second physical specimen using the first negative mold; creating a second negative mold enclosing the second physical specimen therein; producing a casting piece using the second negative mold; and finishing the casting piece to produce the building component.

Rapid prototype three dimensional stereolithography

Methods and apparatus are disclosed for the production of high precision large scale, micro and mini structures using three dimensional stereolithography. The objects formed using these methods have minimal stress between layers and low curl distortion. The objects also have low warpage because no post-cure treatment is necessary. The methods include the use of elevated pressure, elevated temperature, or sequential polymerization of polymer precursor fluid, or a combination of these, in the three dimensional stereolithographic process.

Additively manufactured high temperature objects

Method for producing an object by additively manufacturing a preform of the object from a building material comprising a polymer. The preform is encapsulated with a metal or metal alloy encapsulant that is capable of withstanding temperatures greater than the preform. The encapsulated preform is heated at a predetermined temperature and for a period of time, such that the preform at least partially transmutes into the form of a carbonaceous solid.

Method for manufacturing build material application device for apparatus for additively manufacturing three-dimensional component

To provide a build material application device having improved properties, in particular, properties which can be readily individually specified by a user.SOLUTION: A build material application device (6) is provided for an apparatus (1) for additively manufacturing a three-dimensional component by means of successive layerwise selective irradiation and consolidation of layers of a build material (3) that can be consolidated by means of an energy beam (4). The build material application device (6) is configured to apply a layer of the build material (3) within a build plane (7) of each apparatus (1); and the build material application device (6) includes at least one build material application member (10, 11). The at least one build material application member (10, 11) is at least partially additively manufactured by means of successive layerwise selective irradiation and consolidation of layers of the build material (3) that can be consolidated by means of the energy beam (4).SELECTED DRAWING: Figure 3

Single-scraper bi-directional powder laying device, additive manufacturing equipment and powder laying method

The invention discloses a single-scraper bi-directional powder laying device. The single-scraper bi-directional powder laying device comprises a pushing block and a scraper; the pushing block is located in a sunken section formed in one side of a forming table, the upper surface of the pushing block is flush with the surface of the forming table, and the pushing block can move leftwards and rightwards in the journey of the sunken section under driving of a pushing block driving mechanism; and the scraper can move leftwards and rightwards on a plane of a powder feeding bin, the forming table and the upper surface of the pushing block. The invention further discloses a single-scraper bi-directional powder laying method, specifically, when the scraper conducts a first time of powder laying, the pushing block moves in the sunken section to push powder in the sunken section to the surface of the forming table, and the scraper conducts a second time of powder laying by utilizing the powder. According to the single-scraper bi-directional powder laying device and method, high efficiency of bi-directional power scraping and easy operation and maintenance of the single scraper can be achieved at the same time, hidden danger caused by bi-scraper leveling and low efficiency of the single scraper are avoided, and while bi-directional efficient power laying is ensured, reliability of equipment is greatly improved.

Generation of casting molds by additive manufacturing

A disclosed system includes an additive manufacturing printer that performs a layer by layer three-dimensional printing process generating a casting mold based on a three-dimensional numerical specification. The numerical specification is based on a desired casting shape, including internal features such as hollow areas formed by cores, and is further based on a thermo-mechanical model of a casting process. The numerical specification describes variations in material and geometric properties of one or more layers of the casting mold corresponding to variations in the thermal and mechanical properties of the casting processes, as predicted by the thermo-mechanical model. The system may vary the thickness of features of the casting mold, based on predicted cooling rates, to reduce cooling non-uniformities and to provide for controlled, predictable cooling of the casting. The system may further generate trusses and heat sinks in the mold to respectively strengthen and weaken various features of the mold.

Method for additive manufacturing of printed metal parts by means of prefabricated sand moulds

The invention belongs to the technical field of additive manufacturing, and provides a method for manufacturing printed metal parts through prefabricated sand mold additive manufacturing. Firstly, preparing a prefabricated sand molding piece by a 3D ink-jet printing technology, then printing a metal part at the printing position of the prefabricated sand molding piece by using metal powder, then printing a metal support, and spraying the metal powder on the surface of the prefabricated sand molding piece before printing each time for surface treatment until the molding of the metal part and the metal support is finished. And dissolving the prefabricated sand mold by using a dissolving agent, and finally removing the metal support and the substrate by using machining to obtain the metal part. The invention prevents the defect of printing failure caused by the collapse of a molten pool due to no structural support in the powder feeding/wire feeding printing stage, avoids redundant mechanical processing, reduces the molding cost of metal parts with complex structures, and meets the market demand.

Metallopolymers for additive manufacturing of metal foams

According to one inventive concept, a metallopolymer material includes a plurality of nanoparticles, a base, and water. Each of the nanoparticles includes a plurality of oligomer segments, where oligomer segments are coordinated to adjacent oligomer segments. Each of the oligomer segments includes a thiol and a metal.

System and method for monitoring manufacturing precision in the additive manufacturing of three-dimensional components

The invention relates to a system for monitoring manufacturing precision in the additive manufacturing of three-dimensional components, in which a combined illumination and detection element (12) is provided, which is equipped with a two-dimensional detector array (1) and at least one laser radiation source (4), with which electromagnetic radiation is directed onto a region of a material in powder or paste form, with which at least one region of a three-dimensional component is produced as a result of a locally defined energy input. The detector array (1) is arranged and designed such that speckle arising in/on the surface irradiated by the laser radiation source (4) can be detected in a spatially resolved manner. The speckle signals sensed in a spatially resolved manner using the detector array can be fed to an electronic evaluation and control circuit (3). The electronic evaluation and control circuit (3) is connected to an electronic open- and closed-loop control device which is designed to influence the production process. Thermal speckle excitation can be achieved using a separate energy beam or an energy beam (10) with which the energy input takes place locally in a defined manner on the material in powder or paste form for three-dimensional additive manufacturing.
Method and machine for manufacturing parts by additive manufacturing using a paste material

Systems and methods for measuring radiated thermal energy during an additive manufacturing operation

This disclosure describes various methods and apparatus for characterizing an additive manufacturing process. A method for characterizing the additive manufacturing process can include generating scans of an energy source across a build plane; measuring an amount of energy radiated from the build plane during each of the scans using an optical sensor; determining an area of the build plane traversed during the scans; determining a thermal energy density for the area of the build plane traversed by the scans based upon the amount of energy radiated and the area of the build plane traversed by the scans; mapping the thermal energy density to one or more location of the build plane; determining that the thermal energy density is characterized by a density outside a range of density values; and thereafter, adjusting subsequent scans of the energy source across or proximate the one or more locations of the build plane.

Sliding window screen for reducing resin refilling time in stereolithography

A sliding window is used in a projection-based stereolithographic process to more quickly deliver uncured resin after each curing pass. The sliding window may be configured in different patterns, and includes features for delivering resin and exposing resin to curing radiation. The window screen divides the stereolithographic building area divided into two portions, a light exposure portion for resin curing and a liquid resin refilling portion. The light exposure portion is used to selectively solidify liquid resin, while the liquid resin refilling portion is used to quickly refill liquid resin in order to build additional layers. During the layer fabrication process, a mask image is projected to the tank; however, the projection light only passes through the light exposure portion of the window screen.

Additive manufacturing method using a thermoplastic material having a selected melt index

A method of manufacturing a thermoplastic article, comprising: depositing a plurality of layers of thermoplastic material in a predetermined pattern; and melting the plurality of layers of material to form the article. The thermoplastic material has a melt flow index of 30 g / 10 min to 75 g measured at 230 ° C. and 3.8 kg, or 300 ° C. and 1.2 kg, in accordance with ASTM D1238-04. A method characterized in that it comprises a thermoplastic composition that is / 10 minutes and an article made from the thermoplastic article is disclosed. [Selection figure] None

A kind of conductive photosensitive resin composition for photocuring rapid prototyping

The invention discloses a kind of conductive photosensitive resin compositions for photocuring rapid prototyping, it is grouped as by following mass percent group: epoxy resin 10-30%, acrylate oligomer 5-20%, acrylic ester monomer 5-20%, conductive filler 30-78%, free radical photo-initiation 1-8%, cation light initiator 1-8%.Product has high rigidity, high intensity, certain toughness after photosensitive resin composition solidification of the present invention, while having certain electric conductivity, has filled up in the market for the vacancy of such product electric conductivity demand.Volume contraction is small after present composition solidification, and water absorption rate is low, and the dimensional stability of product is high.

Beryllium-aluminum alloy powder and preparation method and application thereof

The invention provides beryllium-aluminum alloy powder and a preparation method and application thereof. The preparation method can comprise the following steps: placing a beryllium-aluminum alloy rod in a vacuum environment; introducing inert gas into the vacuum environment to replace air, wherein the oxygen content in the vacuum environment after replacement is below 6 ppm; melting the end face of the beryllium-aluminum alloy rod to form a liquid film by using an electric arc; breaking the liquid film into fine droplets by centrifugal force; and cooling to obtain beryllium-aluminum alloy powder. The beryllium-aluminum alloy powder can comprise the beryllium-aluminum alloy powder prepared by the preparation method of the beryllium-aluminum alloy powder. The application may comprise an application in the field of laser or electron beam additive manufacturing, and/or an application in the field of laser or electron beam cladding. The beneficial effects of the invention can include: the preparation method has high production efficiency and low energy consumption; the prepared spherical beryllium-aluminum alloy powder has good sphericity, basically no hollow powder, low oxygen increment in the preparation process and good fluidity, and is a good raw material for 3D printing.
Additive manufacturing machine and additive manufacturing method using such a machine

Three-dimensional printing

Methods and equipment are disclosed relating to three-dimensional printing that include positioning an array of nozzles within a bed of loose solid material, moving the array of nozzles through the bed of loose solid material while injecting a liquid from the array of nozzles in a controlled pattern causing the liquid to react with a portion of the bed of loose solid material forming a solid concrete three-dimensional object. An unreacted portion of the bed of loose solid material may then be separated from the solid three-dimensional object.

Cooling device for arc printing additive manufacturing process

The utility model belongs to the technical field of additive manufacturing equipment, in particular to a cooling device for an arc printing additive manufacturing process, wherein two servo motors are directly connected with a speed reducer and then respectively fixed on two sides above a cooling tank; the lower output shaft of the speed reducer is connected with the screw rod through a coupler and drives the screw rod to rotate; the nut is fixed on the lifting base plate and is in threaded connection with the screw rod, and the screw rod can drive the nut and the lifting base plate to lift in the cooling tank when rotating; cooling liquid is arranged in the cooling tank; and a cooling coil is arranged at the bottom of the cooling tank and is filled with cooling water through a water pump. Through cooling coil's constant temperature water cooling, can in time take away the absorptive heat of coolant liquid to guarantee that the coolant liquid does not solidify, guarantee going on of long-time electric arc printing work, still can guarantee the coolant liquid temperature when printing next time simultaneously, have that cooling efficiency is high, the cooling effect is good, do not influence advantages such as original product organizational performance, realized that electric arc prints simple and efficient cooling of vibration material disk manufacturing process.

Selective control of mechanical properties by construction of stereolithography modeling style

(57) [PROBLEMS] By using an improved curing treatment,   An improved process for forming a three-dimensional object from a photocurable or liquid material is provided. SOLUTION: In order to form an object having mechanical properties corresponding to individual requirements, a photocurable material or a liquid material is cured by using a plurality of different curing methods for shaping a single object, Each of the selected object regions therein is given specific mechanical properties.

Method for producing adhesive-bonding surfaces by means of a 3d printing device

A method for producing an adhesive-bonding surface is proposed. The method comprises providing an adherend having at least one surface which is defined as a surface to be adhesively bonded; or providing a transfer adhesive carrier having at least one, preferably planar, surface as a carrier for an adhesive to be applied, and also applying an adhesive to the at least one surface of the adherend or the transfer adhesive carrier by means of a 3D printing device. The use of a 3D printing device for applying an adhesive to a surface of an adherend or a surface of a transfer adhesive carrier is also proposed, and a method for adhesively bonding two adherends is provided.

Method for regenerating spherical aggregate in laminate molding material, regenerated laminate molding material, and method for producing laminate molded article

In the additive manufacturing using an additive manufacturing material, there is a problem that a part of the additive manufacturing material becomes an additive manufacturing object, and the other part becomes a discarded material. This waste material can be reused as it is as a layered modeling material, but the layered modeling material used in the selective laser sintering method etc. is a waste material as a layered modeling material because its resin component is thermally degraded. When 100% is reused, the shaped product is inferior in strength, has a crushed skin, and inferior in quality. For this reason, in order to maintain the quality of a molded article when reusing a waste material, a large amount of new synthetic resin and spherical aggregate must be added. If it does so, since it will produce in large quantities the waste material which will not be reused for every shaping | molding, it will become impossible in reality to recycle all the waste material.   The method for regenerating a spherical aggregate in an additive manufacturing material according to the present invention is characterized in that a recycled spherical aggregate is obtained by thermally decomposing and removing a synthetic resin in the additive manufacturing material. . IMPROVED METHOD OF PHOTOPOLYMERIZING A RESIN, PREFERABLY FOR 3D PRINTING AN OBJECT BY STEREOLITHOGRAPHY

Additive manufacturing system

The present invention relates to an additive manufacturing system (1) comprising at least one powder based additive manufacturing machine (2) having a build chamber (3), at least one stationary powder storage container (4), and a powder feeding device (6) for feeding powder from the at least one powder storage container (4) into the build chamber (3), characterized in that said build chamber (3), said stationary powder storage container (4) and said powder feeding device (6) form a part of a powder handling system, which is hermetically sealable in a powder-tight manner and to which a movable powder storage container (31) can be connected, said powder handling system being designed to selectively feed powder from the movable powder storage container (31) to the stationary powder storage container (4) or to remove powder from the build chamber (3) in the hermetically sealed state. Moreover, the present invention relates to a Method for feeding powder into and removing powder from a build chamber (3) of at least one additive manufacturing machine (2), characterized in that the feeding and the removal of the powder takes place within a hermetically sealable powder handling system.

Additive manufacturing of metal alloys and metal alloy matrix composites

An additive manufacturing method of producing a metal alloy article may involve: Providing a supply of a metal alloy in powder form; providing a supply of a nucleant material, the nucleant material lowering the nucleation energy required to crystallize the metal alloy; blending the supply of metal alloy powder and nucleant material to form a blended mixture; forming the blended mixture into a first layer; subjecting at least a portion of the first layer to energy sufficient to raise the temperature of the first layer to at least the liquidus temperature of the metal alloy; allowing at least a portion of the first layer to cool to a temperature sufficient to allow the metal alloy to recrystallize; forming a second layer of the blended mixture on the first layer; and repeating the subjecting and allowing steps on the second layer to form an additional portion of the metal alloy article.

System for printing an object and a method for printing an object

A system and a method for printing an object includes a display module arranged to display a two-dimensional representation within a two-dimensional space, the two-dimensional representation being arranged to represent a two-dimensional view of a three-dimensional object within the two-dimensional space; a processing module arranged to transform the two-dimensional representation into a plurality of two-dimensional expressions arranged to individually represent a portion of the three-dimensional object; and a printing module arranged to form the three-dimensional object from a fluid medium arranged to transform its physical state in response to a manipulated illumination exposed thereto, wherein the manipulated illumination exposed to the fluid medium is associated with the plurality of two-dimensional expressions disposed therebetween, and with the inner surface of the printed three-dimensional object being arranged to match the outer surface of a real-world object inserted therein.

A kind of portable 3D printer storage bin

The invention discloses a kind of portable 3D printer storage bin, including casing, case lid and bottom plate, the case lid is hingedly connected to the left side wall top of casing, the right surface of the casing is provided with rising switch and down switch, the right surface top of the casing is provided with clip assembly, the right side wall inner surface of the case lid offers the buckling groove corresponding with clip assembly, and the upper surface of the case lid opens up fluted, and the upper surface of the groove is provided with handheld devices.The present invention is simple in construction, ingenious in design, by using cooperatively for casing and case lid, plays the effect of being effectively protected to protection 3D printer, and by being provided with handheld devices on lid, device is easy to carry；Used cooperatively, can improved the horizontal accuracy of printer with automatic leveling bottom plate, meet the job requirement of 3D printer by bottom plate, the first elevating lever, the second elevating lever, obliquity sensor, controller.

A kind of bi-directional synchronization mixes a 3D printing head

Powder spreading in binder jetting for additive manufacturing

The devices, systems, and methods of the present disclosure are directed to spreading powder to facilitate accurate layer-by-layer fabrication of three-dimensional objects formed through binder jetting. More specifically, a spreader may be moved across a volume defined by a powder box to spread the powder in a layer. As the spreader is moved across the volume, the spreader may vibrate to pack the powder in the volume. By applying this vibration to the powder on a layer-by-layer basis, the resulting three-dimensional object formed through the binder jetting process may have improved density. In turn, such improved density may be useful for forming the three-dimensional objects into finished parts meeting target density standards, which may be particularly useful in the fabrication of metal parts. Further, or instead, applying vibration to the powder may reduce the likelihood of layer-to-layer variations in the three-dimensional object, thus reducing the likelihood of defects in finished parts.

According to at least one aspect of present embodiments, a method for fabrication of shape memory alloy (SMA) based actuators includes multimaterial fabrication of a three-dimensional (3D) printed actuator structure comprising at least soft matrix elastomer material and rigid polymer material, the actuator structure having a SMA wire channel formed therein, pre-straining a SMA wire and inserting the pre-strained SMA wire into the SMA wire channel in the actuator structure while firmly affixing the SMA wire to the printed actuator structure. The method further includes heating the SMA wire until it exceeds its austentite transition temperature and contracts, the contracted SMA wire transferring contraction to the actuator structure, and cooling the SMA wire to release elastic strain energy in the actuator structure.

Combined additive manufacturing method applicable to parts and molds

A combined additive manufacturing method applicable to parts and molds, relating to the field of parts and molds manufacturing by mold-free forming, and comprising the following steps: S1, according to the shape, thickness and dimensional accuracy of the workpiece to be formed, layer processing of the 3D CAD model of said workpiece is performed, to obtain multi-layer slice data; S2, on the basis of the multi-layer slice data, planning of the forming steps is undertaken, and the required numerical control code is generated for each layer; S3, on the basis of the numerical control code for each layer obtained in S2, powder material is stacked layer by layer on a substrate to form the shape that is then further processed by pressure treatment or milling; the layer by layer forming utilizes a numerically-controlled high-speed cold-spray gun to apply the powder material to the designated locations and thus build up the form by stacking. The present method overcomes both the undesired effects of heat processing and the defects of cold-spray stacking.

Dual wire welding or additive manufacturing system and method

Corrosion measurement with multiple compensation
An electrical resistance-type corrosion measuring probe provides an output signal corrected for temperature by computing the ratio of resistance of a test element exposed to a corrosive element to resistance of a reference element protected from the environment. A secondary temperature compensation is provided for by compensating the corrosion output signal for dynamic or short term variation of temperature difference between test and reference elements. The corrosion output signal is compensated for still other environmentally induced errors by measuring temperature gradient between an inner end of the probe within the fluid environment and a portion of the probe outside the corrosive fluid environment. Apparatus and method for raman spectroscopy and microscopy with time domain spectral analysis
An apparatus and method for measuring Raman-type spectra using optical dispersion to convert an optical spectrum into a waveform which can be detected directly in the time domain without the use of a conventional spectrometer. In the example of stimulated Raman spectroscopy, the apparatus and method exposes a sample to a chirped, pulsed probe beam and a Raman pump beam and the resulting Raman spectra is detected by an optical detector in the time domain, and analyzed. Alternatively, the Raman spectra from the probe and pump beams is chirped with a dispersive element prior to detection and analysis. Each probe pulse provides a snapshot of the Raman spectrum that is sampled in time so that neither repetitive waveforms nor static samples are required. Therefore, high speed acquisitions and high throughput assays can be conducted. To facilitate detection, these spectral signals can also be amplified using distributed Raman amplification directly in the dispersive element.

Micro/nano particle reinforced composite solder and preparation method therefor
Micro/nano particle reinforced composite solder used for low temperature brazing and a preparation method therefor, relating to the field of lead-free low temperature brazing solder production. Micro/nano particle reinforced tin-based alloy soldering powder is prepared by dispersing, mixing and atomizing micro/nano-level Cu, Ag and Sb particles with molten tin metal, and is then mixed with SnBi low melting point alloy soldering powder and a conventional flux to form micro/nano particle reinforced composite solder; when soldering at a low temperature below 200°C, the tin atoms in the molten micro/nano particle reinforced tin-based alloy form inter-metallic compounds on a bonding pad prior to the SnBi low melting point alloy, and micro/nano particles are dispersed in solder joints and form a "barrier effect", which blocks atomic precipitation in the SnBi and bonding to the bonding pad, thus inhibiting the growth of an enriched Bi layer; the described solder solves the problems of brittle solder joints and unreliability in lead-free low temperature brazing, and is thus very suitable for low temperature lead-free soldering used for fine pitch micro solder joints.

Method for short-period rapid detection of degradability of plastic-based material
The invention provides a method for short-period rapid detection of degradability of a plastic-based material. The method comprises the following steps: pre-treating, drying and cutting the plastic-based material, performing dissolution and alkaline aqueous solution hydrolysis, cleaning a precipitate, and drying and weighing the precipitate; if the mass fraction of the precipitate is less than or equal to 5%, determining that the degradability meets requirements; if the mass fraction of the precipitate is more than 5%, hydrolyzing the precipitate after basic hydrolysis by using an acidic aqueous solution, calculating the mass fraction of insoluble matters, and if the mass fraction of the insoluble matters is less than or equal to 5%, determining that the degradability meets the requirements; and if the mass fraction of the precipitate is more than 5%, determining that the degradability does not meet the requirements. The method for detecting the degradability of the plastic-based material, provided by the invention, is simple, convenient, quick and efficient, has good reliability and applicability, and can be used as or used for replacing a conventional means for detecting the degradability of a biodegradable plastic.

Gas well underground rotational flow atomization water drainage and gas recovery device

Light input ic
PURPOSE: To improve linearity of output current of a silicon photo diode at low illuminance, by applying a connection method which eliminates potential differential on each junction side of a silicon photo diode.  
     CONSTITUTION: An anode electrode of a silicon photo diode 10 is connected to a non-inverting input terminal of an operational amplifier 11 and a P-type semiconductor substrate. A cathode electrode is connected to an inverting input terminal of the operational amplifier 11 and a cathode of a logarithm compression diode 12. The linearity of output current at low illuminance is thereby improved.

Method for fabricating micro pattern on a plastic substrate

A method for forming a micro pattern on a plastic substrate is provided to prevent generation of a concavo-convex on a surface of a plastic substrate by filling a metal micro pattern in the plastic substrate. A metal thin film is formed on a plastic substrate(1) for forming a metal micro pattern. A metal pattern(9) of a thin film type is formed on the plastic substrate by a photolithography process. A photoresist of a fixed thickness is uniformly coated on the metal thin film. A mask having a fixed pattern is arranged on the photoresist coated on the surface of the metal thin film. An exposure process is performed. The photoresist pattern is formed on the metal thin film by melting the photoresist of a part having a weak coupling force by a developing solution. A micro pattern is formed by etching the metal thin film with the photoresist pattern. The photoresist pattern remaining from the metal micro pattern is removed. An adhesion prevention layer is arranged on a full surface of the plastic substrate including the metal micro pattern. The metal micro pattern is filled with an inner part of the plastic substrate by performing a heating process and a pressurization process.

Method for producing a crown for an implant abutment

A method of producing a crown for a custom implant abutment is carried out as follows. The method begins by preparing a patient's existing dental structures, viz., positioning a dental implant in the patient's mouth. Using a scanner device and associated modeling software, a first 3D model is obtained of a sufficiently large portion of an implant abutment to be attached to the implant. This scan is performed extra-orally. Preferably, the sufficiently large portion is that portion of the abutment bounded by a margin curve.  After the implant abutment is attached to the implant (intra-orally), the scanner is used to obtain a second 3D model of the implant abutment attached to the implant (i.e., an intra-oral scan). Using the modeling software, the first 3D model is then aligned to the second 3D model. Thereafter, a boundary curve on the first 3D model is identified. Using the boundary curve to trim the first 3D model, the system then produces a third 3D model. Using the boundary curve, the third 3D model and the second 3D model, the system then creates a fourth 3D model, which is a model of a virtual dental item. Using a computer-assisted milling machine, the model of the virtual dental item is then used to produce an actual crown, which is then attached to the implant to complete the process.

Method for manufacturing gate electrode of semiconductor device

The present invention relates to a method for forming a T-type gate electrode of a high-speed semiconductor device such as HEMT, which is mainly used in high-speed logic circuits such as low noise receivers, power amplifiers, and millimeter-wave band MMICs in X-band or higher frequencies. On the other hand, in such a device, a pattern having a short gate length and a wide cross-sectional area is required simultaneously. In the conventional photolithography method, an electron beam lithography technique has been mainly used due to a lack of resolution to form a fine line width of a gate channel. However, the method of using the electron beam requires a lot of exposure time in spite of the high resolution, resulting in a decrease in productivity. Therefore, in the present invention, unlike the conventional process, after depositing a silicon oxide film or a silicon nitride film on a single resist pattern, a dummy pattern corresponding to the gate bridge is formed using the same, and then the gate electrode is placed in place of the dummy pattern. By forming the bridge portion, a process for improving the resolution is unnecessary, and a method of forming a gate electrode having a very small line width (hundreds of microns) by controlling the thickness of the silicon nitride film is provided.

Epidermal sensor system and process

Epidermal electronics are sensors with mechanical properties matching human epidermis. Their manufacturing process includes photolithography and dry and wet etching within clean room facilities. The high cost of manpower, materials, photo masks, and facilities greatly hinders the commercialization potential of disposable epidermal electronics. In contrast, an embodiment of the invention includes a low cost, high throughput, bench top "cut and paste" method to complete the freeform manufacture of epidermal sensor system (ESS) in minutes. This versatile method works for many types of thin metal and polymeric sheets and is compatible with many tattoo adhesives or medical tapes. The resultant ESS is highly multimaterial and multifunctional and may measure ECG, EMG, skin temperature, skin hydration, as well as respiratory rate. Also, a stretchable planar coil made of serpentine ribbons can be used as a wireless strain gauge and/or a near field communication (NFC) antenna. Other embodiments are described herein.

PURPOSE: To realize, in one photolithography process, a double structure of source and drain consisting of low-concentration and high-concentration layers essential for a MOS device having a high dielectric strength and to shorten the manufacturing process of a wafer, by positively utilizing the side etching of a gate electrode film.  
     CONSTITUTION: A thermal oxide film 5 is grown on one surface of a silicon semiconductor substrate 1. Then, aluminium 9 for gate electrode film is adhered on the whole surface thereof. Patterns of a photoresist 2 having a high concentration are formed for selectively etching the aluminium 9. The aluminium 9 is selectively wet-etched by using the photoresist 2 as a mask. The aluminium 9 is also side etched due to the isotropy of the wet etching. After finishing the wet etching, ions are implanted by using the photoresist as a mask to form a high-concentration layer 7. The photoresist is then peeled off, and ions are implanted by using the aluminium 9 as a mask to form a low-concentration layer 8. In this manner, source and drain regions having a double structure consisting of the low-concentration and high-concentration layers 8 and 7 can be obtained.

Method of making ohmic contacts in thin-film devices on amorphous undoped semiconductors

FIELD: physics, semiconductors. 
     SUBSTANCE: invention relates to electronic engineering and can be used in making ohmic contacts in thin-film field-effect transistors, memory elements, solar cells based on undoped amorphous hydrogenated silicon or other amorphous semiconductors. The method of making ohmic contacts in thin-film devices on undoped amorphous semiconductors involves depositing a semiconductor film onto a substrate, formation of a masking dielectric layer, photolithography for opening windows in the dielectric layer and sputtering metal electrodes with subsequent photolithography on the metal. Immediately before sputtering the metal electrodes, the semiconductor film undergoes ion bombardment with inert gas ions, e.g. argon, through windows opened in the dielectric layer. 
     EFFECT: method avoids thermal annealing at hydrogen effusion temperature, which may affect electrophysical parametres of the film of amorphous semiconductor, and enables formation of a "broken" layer on the metal-semiconductor junction to create surface ohmic contacts to film-type undoped amorphous semiconductors.

Near-field photolithographic masks and photolithography; nanoscale patterning techniques; apparatus and method therefor

A new field of technology, near-field photolithography, is proposed. In near-field photolithography, an opaque pattern having a nanoscale resolution is made using a modified scanning tunneling microscope to deposit the opaque material on an electrically conductive material. A transparent sheet of indium tin oxide is patterned with a plurality of opaque copper deposits having a nanoscale resolution. The patterned indium tin oxide is then used as a photolithographic mask in the optical near-field. Near-field resolution is not diffraction limited, and near-field photolithography is able to pattern objects with sub-wavelength resolution. As a result, smaller semiconductor microchips can be manufactured and a new nanotechnology, e.g., nanomachines, can be developed. The scanning tunneling microscope (STM) is used as an "electrochemical paintbrush" to transfer the copper from a massive copper supply to the STM electrode tip and then to the ITO surface without degrading the STM tip.

Polymeric modifying agents

Novel modifying agents contain a sharply-melting crystalline polymer ingredient, preferably a side chain crystalline (SCC) ingredient, and an active chemical ingredient. Such modifying agents, especially when in the form of particles, can be placed in contact with a matrix, will not modify the mix below the crystalline melting point T p , but will rapidly modify the matrix above T p . The active chemical ingredient can react with the matrix, catalyze a reaction of the matrix, or inhibit a reaction of the matrix. Particularly useful compositions are polymer precursors which are storage-stable at low temperatures but which are rapidly converted to crosslinked resins when heated to temperatures above T p , optionally in the presence of light.

Method and apparatus for constructing a three-dimensional surface of predetermined shape and color

A coating of a pressure sensitive adhesive is then applied to one side of the sheet material. Areas on the sheet material outside of the profiles are then removed and discarded so as to leave a series of unconnected planar elements, each planar element having an edge shape or outline corresponding to a cross-section of the surface with the color profile itself forming at least a color border or margin on the surface of its respective planar element around the edge. The planar elements are then stacked one on top of the other in proper alignment and sequence and individually laminated together to produce the desired three-dimensional surface. When viewed, the entire surface appears to be colored even though the color is applied only along one side of the planar elements and not along the edges of the planar elements. Autocatalytic coating method

Autocatalytic plating is a form of electrode-less plating in which a metal, for example, cobalt, nickel, gold, silver or copper, is deposited onto a substrate via a chemical reduction process. Coatings derived from this process are usually more uniform and adherent than from other processes and can be applied to unusually shaped surfaces. Non-metallic surfaces can also be coated via this process following suitable sensitisation of the substrate. Autocatalytic deposition is used to coat whole surfaces and the formation of metal patterns requires additional and costly additional processing such as photolithography and etching. This invention therefore provides a method of preparing a substrate material for subsequent autocatalytic deposition of a metal coating in a pre-determined pattern.

PRUVISION OF PLASTIC INSULATION UPON AN ELECTRICAL CONDUCTOR     Abstract of the Disclosure Providing plastic insulation on an electrical conductor in which the conductor, enclosed in an oversize tube, is passed through a fluid and through an annular crystal of a piezoelectric material immersed in the fluid.  During passage of tube and conductor, the crystal is subjected to electric current to cause alternating expansion and contraction of the crystal.  Pressure shockwaves are produced by this expansion and contraction and the shockwaves are focussed upon the pass line of the tube and conductor to cause localized inward deformations of the tube into engagement with the conductor to form spaced annular depressions in the tube. Particulate material and method for producing same, and transparent resin composition

Sloped metal features for cooling hotspots in stacked-die packages

Heat dissipation techniques include using metal features having one or more slanted or otherwise laterally-extending aspects. The metal features include, for example, tilted metal pillars, or metal bodies or fillets having an angled or sloping sidewall, or other metal features that extend both vertically and laterally. Such metal features increase the effective heat transfer area significantly by spreading heat in the in-plane (lateral) direction, in addition to the vertical direction. In some embodiments, slanted trenches are formed in photoresist/mold material deposited over a lower die, using photolithography and a multi-angle lens, or by laser drilling mold material deposited over the lower die. The trenches are then filled with metal. In other embodiments, metal features are printed on the lower die, and then molding material is deposited over the printed features. In any such cases, heat is conducted from a lower die to an upper die and/or an integrated heat spreader.

Method for removing impurity in mica powder

PURPOSE:To improve impurity efficiency by rotating mica powder together with the liquid present in a vessel and by removing the powder accounting for a specific proportion of the total mica powder and existing at the central part of the bottom of the vessel. CONSTITUTION:A bottomed cylindrical vessel 1 is charged with mica powder 2 having an aspect ratio of >=10 and a mean particle diameter of >=5mum containing 0.001-90wt.% of impurities 4 such as minerals, metallic powder, magnetic materials and/or speck materials and a liquid 3 with a density of 0.7-2.5 g/cm<3> such as water at the weight ratio of the mica powder 2/liquid 3 of (1:3) to (1:50). A revolving means 10 such as blade is revolved at 0.5-100rpm for 0.2-10 min to rotate both the powder 2 and liquid 3 smoothly. Subsequently, the mica powder rich in the impurities 4 is gathered at the center of the bottom 5 corresponding to 1-10wt.% of the total mica powder 2 and is discharged out of the vessel 1 through a discharge pipe 6 by opening a valve 7 for a specified time. The mica powder 2 and the liquid 3 are taken out separately, the liquid 3 contained in the mica powder 2 is separated by e.g. centrifugation followed by drying the resultant mica powder 2, thus obtaining high-purity mica powder 2 low in the content of the impurities 4.

Printable compositions comprising polymers and polymeric components, articles, and methods for producing articles thereof

The present disclosure provides printable compositions. The printable composition comprises a polymer, a polymerizable component, a fugitive solvent, a photoinitiator, and optionally an inhibitor. The present disclosure also provides an article that includes an integral blend of a thermosetting polymer and a second polymer that is different than the thermosetting polymer. Further, the present disclosure provides a method of making an article. The method comprises (i) providing a printable composition, (ii) selectively curing the printable composition to form a gelled article, and (iii) temporarily from the gelled article. Removing at least a portion of the selective solvent. The method may optionally further comprise (iv) curing the unpolymerized polymerizable component remaining before or after step (iii). Receiving a digital object containing data defining multiple layers of an article by a manufacturing device having one or more processors, wherein the article is 8-50% by weight (inclusive) thermoset. Receiving an integral blend of a polymer and a second polymer that is different from the thermosetting polymer in the range of 30 to 90% by weight inclusive, wherein the weight percent is based on the total weight of the article; Also provided is a method that includes producing an article based on a digital object using a manufacturing device with an additive manufacturing process. A system is provided that includes a display that displays a 3D model of an article and one or more processors that cause a 3D printer to create a physical object of the article depending on the 3D model selected by the user.

Polyester compositions containing cyclobutanediol having a certain combination of inherent viscosity and moderate glass transition temperature and articles made therefrom

Described are polyesters comprising (a) a dicarboxylic acid component comprising terephthalic acid residues; optionally, aromatic dicarboxylic acid residues or aliphatic dicarboxylic acid residues; 2,2,4,4-tetramethyl-1 ,3- cyclobutanediol residues; and cyclohexanedimethanol residues. The polyesters may be manufactured into articles such as fibers, films, bottles or sheets.

Treatment of skeletal voids with implantable substrate hydrated with bone marrow concentrate

The invention is directed to a bone void filler comprising a scaffold or matrix. The scaffold or matrix may include a porous inorganic matrix component and/or a 3D-printed implantable device. The bone void filler may include a cellular component containing cells, some of which are capable of making extracellular matrix resembling native bone tissue. The bone void filler may include an organic matrix, such as, an organic biopolymer that aids in cell retention and renders the scaffold or matrix moldable. The bone void filler may include growth factors and/or cytokines. The bone void filler may include a clotting agent.

Fabricating method of liquid crystal display device

PURPOSE: A method for manufacturing an LCD(Liquid Crystal Display) is provided to divide a pattern with wide width into patterns having narrow width and then form a resist pattern uniformly, thereby capable of preventing the etching badness caused by the resist with uneven thickness. CONSTITUTION: After coating a buffer layer(103) such as metal, an organic film or silicon on a substrate(101), grooves(105a-105c) are formed on the substrate(101) through a photolithography process, thereby forming a cliche(100). Since patterns designed to the same layer are various, the sizes of the grooves(105a-105c) are also various. The first groove(105a) forms an optimum print pattern. When the line width of the first groove(105a) is d1, the second groove(105b) has a line width of d2 which is narrower than d1 of the first groove(105a). The third groove(105c) has a line width of d3 which is wider than d1 of the first groove(105a). The third groove(105c) is formed by two or more first groove(105a) separated with a predetermined interval(w). When a resist having high viscosity and high energy is used, the interval(w) must be reduced.

Quick dismounting device of circuit board shaping

The utility model discloses a quick dismounting device of circuit board shaping, concretely relates to circuit board technical field, including splint one, the top surface of splint one is seted up flutedly, and one side of splint one is provided with splint two, splint one all overlaps with the outside of splint two and is equipped with cover block one, and splint one all overlaps with one side that the outside of splint two is close to cover block one and is equipped with cover block two, the inside of recess is provided with adjustment mechanism, adjustment mechanism includes spring one, the top fixedly connected with location arch of spring one. The utility model discloses a set up adjustment mechanism, realized the fixed to splint one, locating hole and screw one have all set up threely, and the circuit board of different specifications is adapted to the distance between adjustable splint one and the splint two, has improved the rapid prototyping efficiency of circuit board, has set up stop gear, and stopper and rubber pad can be fixed the lateral wall of circuit board, can avoid the phenomenon that the circuit board appears dropping.

chemical process scheduling system, conical fluid distributor, millirreator support structure and millirreator

The present invention relates to a chemical process scheduling system that provides fluid parallelization and the use of a plurality of millirreators in a chemical process, a conical fluid dispenser (1) manufactured by additive manufacturing that receives and distributes fluids. , a millirreator support structure accommodating a plurality of millirreators and a millirreator (5), aimed at increasing fluid volume in chemical reactions and processes.
